title: Motion In a Straight Line Test - 1 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: A bird moves along a straight path for 4 s. Its speed at time t seconds is $|t - 2|$ m/s. The total distance travelled is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A car travels with a speed of 30 m/s for one-third of the time and with a speed of 15 m/s for the remaining time. The average speed of the car is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A car goes from A to B with a speed of 20 km h-1 and comes back from B to A with a speed of 30 km h-1. The average speed for the complete trip is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A car runs at constant speed on a circular track of radius 100 m taking 62.8 s on each lap. What is the average speed and average velocity on each complete lap? ($\pi = 3.14$)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An object goes 6 m towards north, then 8 m towards east, and then 10 m vertically upward. What is the magnitude of its resultant displacement from the starting point?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An aeroplane flies from $P(-4\text{m}, -5\text{m}, +8\text{m})$ to $Q(7\text{m}, -2\text{m}, -3\text{m})$ in $xyz$ coordinate system. The displacement vector of aeroplane is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A scooter moves in a straight line. It covers one fourth distance with $v_1$, two fourth with $v_2$ and remaining with speed $v_3$. The average speed of scooter is
questions.subject: Physics

options: If displacement covered of a particle is zero, then distance covered may or may not be zero.
If the distance covered is zero, then the displacement must be zero.
The numerical value of ratio of displacement to distance is equal to or less than one.
The numerical value of the ratio of velocity to speed is always less than one.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle starts from the origin, goes along the X-axis to the point $(20\ m, 0)$ and then returns along the same line to the point $(-20m, 0)$. Find the distance and displacement of the particle during the trip.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body covers one-third of the distance with a velocity $v_1$, the second one-third of the distance with a velocity $v_2$, and the last one-third of the distance with a velocity $v_3$. The average velocity is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A truck travels a distance $A$ to $B$ at a speed of $40 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ and returns to $A$ at a speed of $50 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$, then what is the average velocity of the whole journey?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A car covers a distance of 2 km in 2.5 minute, if it covers half of the distance with speed $40 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$. the rest distance it will cover with speed
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A motor car covers $\frac{1}{3}$ rd part of total distance with $v_1=10 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$, second $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{rd}$ part with $v_2=20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$ and rest $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{rd}$ part with $v_3=60 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$. What is the average speed of the car?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle moving in a straight line covers half the distance with speed of $12 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. The other half of the distance is covered in two equal time intervals with speed of $4.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $7.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ respectively. The average speed of the particle during this motion is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A moving car possesses average velocities of $5 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}, 10 \mathrm{m} \mathrm{s}^{-1}$, and $15 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ in the first, second, and third seconds, respectively. What is the total distance covered by the car in these 3 s?
questions.subject: Physics
